question:
Find polynomials $f(x)$,$g(x)$, and $h(x)$, if they exist, such
that for all $x$,
\[
|f(x)|-|g(x)|+h(x) = \begin{cases} -1 & \mbox{if $x<-1$} \\
                     3x+2 & \mbox{if $-1 \leq x \leq 0$} \\
                     -2x+2 & \mbox{if $x>0$.}
                     \end{cases}
\]
solution:
Note that if $r(x)$ and $s(x)$ are any two functions, then
\[ \max(r,s) = (r+s + |r-s|)/2.\]
Therefore, if $F(x)$ is the given function, we have
\begin{align*}
F(x)\ &= \max\{-3x-3,0\}-\max\{5x,0\}+3x+2 \\
      &= (-3x-3+|3x+3|)/2 \\
      & \qquad - (5x + |5x|)/2 + 3x+2 \\
      &= |(3x+3)/2| - |5x/2| -x + \frac{1}{2},
\end{align*}
so we may set $f(x)=(3x+3)/2$, $g(x) = 5x/2$, and $h(x)=-x+\frac{1}{2}$.

descriptive_long.question:
Find polynomials $\polyfunf(\variablex)$, $\polyfung(\variablex)$, and $\polyfunh(\variablex)$, if they exist, such that for all $\variablex$,
\[
|\polyfunf(\variablex)|-|\polyfung(\variablex)|+\polyfunh(\variablex)=\begin{cases}-1&\mbox{if $\variablex<-1$}\\3\variablex+2&\mbox{if $-1\leq\variablex\leq0$}\\-2\variablex+2&\mbox{if $\variablex>0$.}\end{cases}
\]
descriptive_long.solution:
Note that if $\funcrr(\variablex)$ and $\funcss(\variablex)$ are any two functions, then
\[\max(\funcrr,\funcss)=(\funcrr+\funcss+|\funcrr-\funcss|)/2.\]
Therefore, if $\bigfuncf(\variablex)$ is the given function, we have
\begin{align*}
\bigfuncf(\variablex)\ &=\max\{-3\variablex-3,0\}-\max\{5\variablex,0\}+3\variablex+2\\
&=(-3\variablex-3+|3\variablex+3|)/2\\
&\qquad-(5\variablex+|5\variablex|)/2+3\variablex+2\\
&=|(3\variablex+3)/2|-|5\variablex/2|-\variablex+\frac12,
\end{align*}
so we may set $\polyfunf(\variablex)=(3\variablex+3)/2$, $\polyfung(\variablex)=5\variablex/2$, and $\polyfunh(\variablex)=-\variablex+\frac12$.

descriptive_long_confusing.question:
Find polynomials $moondust(lanterns)$,$treasure(lanterns)$, and $sunshine(lanterns)$, if they exist, such
that for all $lanterns$,
\[
|moondust(lanterns)|-|treasure(lanterns)|+sunshine(lanterns) = \begin{cases} -1 & \mbox{if $lanterns<-1$} \\
                     3lanterns+2 & \mbox{if $-1 \leq lanterns \leq 0$} \\
                     -2lanterns+2 & \mbox{if $lanterns>0$.}
                     \end{cases}
\]
descriptive_long_confusing.solution:
Note that if $biscotti(lanterns)$ and $pinecones(lanterns)$ are any two functions, then
\[ \max(biscotti,pinecones) = (biscotti+pinecones + |biscotti-pinecones|)/2.\]
Therefore, if $waterfall(lanterns)$ is the given function, we have
\begin{align*}
waterfall(lanterns)\ &= \max\{-3lanterns-3,0\}-\max\{5lanterns,0\}+3lanterns+2 \\
      &= (-3lanterns-3+|3lanterns+3|)/2 \\
      & \qquad - (5lanterns + |5lanterns|)/2 + 3lanterns+2 \\
      &= |(3lanterns+3)/2| - |5lanterns/2| -lanterns + \frac{1}{2},
\end{align*}
so we may set $moondust(lanterns)=(3lanterns+3)/2$, $treasure(lanterns) = 5lanterns/2$, and $sunshine(lanterns)=-lanterns+\frac{1}{2}$.

descriptive_long_misleading.question:
Find polynomials $antipoly(unchanging)$,$randomness(unchanging)$, and $flatline(unchanging)$, if they exist, such
that for all $unchanging$,
\[
|antipoly(unchanging)|-|randomness(unchanging)|+flatline(unchanging) = \begin{cases} -1 & \mbox{if $unchanging<-1$} \\
                     3unchanging+2 & \mbox{if $-1 \leq unchanging \leq 0$} \\
                     -2unchanging+2 & \mbox{if $unchanging>0$.}
                     \end{cases}
\]

garbled_string.question:
Find polynomials $oqpdzmla(lbgrtkhs)$,$kbvtryhs(lbgrtkhs)$, and $zjfdmnel(lbgrtkhs)$, if they exist, such
that for all $lbgrtkhs$,
\[
|oqpdzmla(lbgrtkhs)|-|kbvtryhs(lbgrtkhs)|+zjfdmnel(lbgrtkhs) = \begin{cases} -1 & \mbox{if $lbgrtkhs<-1$} \\
                     3lbgrtkhs+2 & \mbox{if $-1 \leq lbgrtkhs \leq 0$} \\
                     -2lbgrtkhs+2 & \mbox{if $lbgrtkhs>0$.}
                     \end{cases}
\]

kernel_variant.question:
Find (if they exist) real-coefficient polynomials \(f(x),\,g(x),\,h(x)\) such that
\[
|f(x)|-|g(x)|+h(x)=
\begin{cases}
-5x-49 & \text{if }x<-4,\\[4pt]
6x-5 & \text{if }-4\le x\le 2,\\[4pt]
-3x+13 & \text{if }x>2.
\end{cases}
\]
kernel_variant.solution:
Let
F(x)=\begin{cases}-5x-49 & (x<-4),\\
6x-5    & (-4\le x\le 2),\\
-3x+13  & (x>2).\end{cases}

----------------------------------------------------------------------
Step 1 -  Writing F with max-terms

Observe that the graph changes formula only at x=-4 and x=2.  Set
\[
A(x)=-11x-44 \qquad(=0\text{ when }x=-4),\qquad B(x)=9x-18 \qquad(=0\text{ when }x=2).
\]
On the three regions we have
\[
\begin{array}{c|ccc}
 & x<-4 & -4\le x\le 2 & x>2\\\hline
A(x) & >0 & \le 0 & <0\\
B(x) & <0 & \le 0 & >0
\end{array}
\]
Hence
\[
F(x)=\max\{A(x),0\}-\max\{B(x),0\}+6x-5.
\]
----------------------------------------------------------------------
Step 2 -  Eliminating the maxima

For any functions r,s we have \(\max\{r,s\}=\tfrac12\,(r+s+|r-s|)\).  With s\equiv0 this gives
\[\max\{u,0\}=\frac{u+|u|}{2}.\]
Applying it to the two max-terms,
\[
F(x)=\frac{A(x)+|A(x)|}{2}-\frac{B(x)+|B(x)|}{2}+6x-5
      =\frac{|A(x)|}{2}-\frac{|B(x)|}{2}+\Bigl(\frac{A(x)-B(x)}{2}+6x-5\Bigr).
\]
----------------------------------------------------------------------
Step 3 -  Grouping as |f|-|g|+h

Define
\[
\boxed{\,f(x)=\dfrac{A(x)}{2}= -\frac{11x+44}{2}\,},\qquad
\boxed{\,g(x)=\dfrac{B(x)}{2}= \frac{9x-18}{2}\,},\qquad
\boxed{\,h(x)=\frac{A(x)-B(x)}{2}+6x-5\,}.
\]
Since
\[h(x)=\frac{-11x-44-9x+18}{2}+6x-5=-4x-18,\]
all three functions are polynomials.  Finally,
\[
|f(x)|-|g(x)|+h(x)=\frac{|A(x)|}{2}-\frac{|B(x)|}{2}+\Bigl(\frac{A(x)-B(x)}{2}+6x-5\Bigr)=F(x),
\]
so the requested polynomials are
\[
\boxed{\,f(x)= -\frac{11}{2}x-22,\quad g(x)=\frac{9}{2}x-9,\quad h(x)=-4x-18\,}.
\]
These indeed satisfy the given piecewise equation, completing the proof.

core_steps: Rewrite the target piecewise-linear function as a combination of max{linear,linear} terms whose breakpoints coincide with those of the piecewise definition.
Apply max(a,b) = (a + b + |a − b|)/2 to convert each max into an expression involving an absolute value.
Re-group the resulting expression so it becomes |f(x)| − |g(x)| + h(x) with f, g, h polynomial.

slot3.description: Slope of the leftmost linear segment

slot4.description: Intercept of the leftmost linear segment

slot5.description: Slope of the middle linear segment

slot6.description: Intercept of the middle linear segment

slot7.description: Slope of the rightmost linear segment

slot8.description: Intercept of the rightmost linear segment

slot9.description: Coefficient of x in the subtracted max term (g-term) 5x
